title: Suspicious phishing emails targeting local businesses
description: Multiple small businesses on Elmwood Avenue reported receiving phishing emails impersonating the Buffalo Niagara Partnership. The emails contain malicious links asking for banking credentials. At least 5 businesses in Elmwood Village have been targeted in the past 48 hours.

title: Street light outage on Delaware Avenue
description: Several street lights on Delaware Avenue between North Street and Allen Street have been reported as non-functional for the past three nights. Residents and visitors to the Allentown district have expressed concern about pedestrian safety during evening hours.

title: Data breach at regional healthcare provider
description: Kaleida Health announced a data breach affecting approximately 12,000 patients across Western New York. Exposed data may include names, dates of birth, and insurance information. The breach occurred through a compromised third-party vendor system.

title: Increased package theft reports in North Buffalo
description: Over the past two weeks, 15 incidents of package theft have been reported in North Buffalo near Hertel Avenue. Most incidents occur between 2-5 PM on weekdays. Ring camera footage shows a recurring white van in the area between Colvin Blvd and Starin Avenue.

title: Someone's dog keeps barking all night
description: OMG I can't sleep because of this annoying dog on Bidwell Parkway!!! It barks ALL NIGHT. This is ridiculous and nobody does anything about it. I'm so tired of this neighborhood. Moving out ASAP!!!

title: New ransomware variant targeting home networks
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware strain called 'HomeLocker' that specifically targets consumer-grade routers with default configurations. The malware spreads through compromised IoT devices and encrypts connected NAS drives. Users with default router passwords are most at risk.

title: Water main break advisory - boil water notice
description: Due to a water main break at the intersection of Main St and Utica in the University District, residents in the affected area should boil water before consumption until further notice. Repairs are expected to take 24-48 hours. The city has set up free water distribution at the Delavan-Grider Community Center.

title: This city is going downhill fast
description: Everything is terrible here. Crime is up, schools are bad, and my neighbor is annoying. Can't believe I have to live here. When will the city council do something??? Taxes keep going up and services keep getting worse. Total disaster.

title: Free community CPR training this Saturday
description: The Buffalo Fire Department is offering free CPR and first aid training at the downtown community center on Pearl Street this Saturday from 9 AM to 1 PM. Registration is required and spots are limited to 30 participants. Contact the fire department or register online.

title: Scam calls impersonating IRS reported in area
description: Multiple residents across Erie County have reported receiving phone calls from individuals claiming to be IRS agents demanding immediate payment via gift cards. The callers threaten arrest if payment is not made. The IRS confirms these are scams and reminds the public that the IRS will never demand immediate payment over the phone.

title: Vehicle break-ins near Delaware Park
description: Police report a series of vehicle break-ins in the Delaware Park parking areas near the Ring Road over the past weekend. 8 vehicles were targeted, with thieves primarily stealing electronics and bags left visible in cars. Police recommend not leaving valuables in vehicles.

title: WiFi at the coffee shop is so slow today
description: Went to Spot Coffee on Elmwood and the wifi is impossibly slow. Can't even load a webpage. This is unacceptable for a place that charges $7 for a latte. Maybe I'll go to Starbucks instead. UPDATE: turns out my laptop was the problem lol

title: Elderly resident scam alert - fake utility workers
description: Two individuals posing as National Grid workers have been going door-to-door in the Kaisertown senior community. They claim to need access to check meters but instead case homes for valuables. One resident reported a missing jewelry box after their visit. Residents should demand official ID and call National Grid to verify.

title: Public WiFi vulnerability at Buffalo & Erie County Library
description: A security audit revealed that the public WiFi network at the Buffalo & Erie County Public Library downtown branch has been configured without proper network isolation. This means users on the same network could potentially intercept each other's unencrypted traffic. The library has been notified and plans to update their network configuration by end of week.

title: Emergency weather alert - lake effect snow warning
description: The National Weather Service has issued a lake effect snow warning for the Southtowns and South Buffalo through tomorrow afternoon. Accumulations of 18-24 inches expected. Residents should avoid unnecessary travel. Emergency shelters available at the Buffalo Convention Center at Canalside.
